observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Property Custodian to: (i) prepare the IIRUP for all properties found to be unserviceable during the inventory taking and facilitate the disposal process following the procedures under the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments to prevent further deterioration of the equipment, to decongest the storage area for unserviceable properties and to realize additional income therefrom, if any, to the Municipality; and                 (ii) revisit COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other property inventory related guidelines and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper dropping of the said properties in the books.
observations.reason: The IIRUP was not yet prepared by the Management due to oversight of the concerned municipal officials/employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Treasurer to (i) generate an efficient and effective collection scheme and strictly impose compliance of collection thereon; and (ii) encourage the borrowers to pay their matured/due and demandable obligation;
Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification of loans receivable accounts to determine its conditions that would affect its collectability; and
MSWDO to (i) properly evaluate and assess the application and loan proposal of the loan proponents in accordance with the required guidelines should the Municipality implement financial assistance or other livelihood programs in the future; and (ii) ensure the propriety and completeness of the proponents’ supporting documents before processing their application for loan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Property Custodian to prepare the IIRUP for all properties found to be unserviceable during the inventory taking and facilitate the disposal thereof to prevent further deterioration of the equipment, to decongest the storage area for unserviceable properties and to realize additional income therefrom, if any, to the Municipality;
Inventory Team to: (i) properly conduct the physical count of PPE; and (ii) duly accomplish the RPCPPE and ensure that all the relevant details are indicated therein; and
Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that the PPE accounts only include items costing P50,000.00 and above, in compliance with Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry for the derecognition of unserviceable properties for an accurate and fair presentation of the financial statements at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Health Officer to properly plan the disposition/distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, to prevent wastage of government resources;
BAC to comply with the (i) provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances, particularly the appropriate method to be used on the procurements of COVID-19 related PPAs; and (ii) posting requirements in all instances of the alternative method of procurement to enhance transparency and to establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process; and
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate refund/ remittance of the unexpended balances of the completed projects to the concerned SAs or the BTr, pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
LGU Focal Person to (i) provide a written justification on the erasures and alterations in the payroll and supporting documents and why TUPAD payments were released to a representative, without the required TUPAD ID of the beneficiary; and henceforth (ii) administer the proper implementation of the program in accordance with the provisions of the MOA and DOLE DO No. 219, series of 2020; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the payrolls are supported with complete supporting documents, required under the MOA, DOLE DO No. 219, series of 2020 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding the completeness and propriety of bidding documents, invitation to observers, and posting requirements, among others, to enhance transparency and to establish the regularity of the procurement process; and
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under Sections 9.1 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments to ensure compliance with 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to establish the legality, validity, and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to:
Direct the concerned contractor/s to correct and rectify the noted deficiencies on the three projects, otherwise require the payment of the corresponding cost of the deficiencies noted; and
Submit the approved Suspension Order and/or Time Extension on the project, Construction of Barangay Ninikat ng Pag-asa Drainage Canal along G. Del Pilar Street, otherwise impose upon the contractor liquidated damages for the 31 calendar days delayed.

We recommended the Management for the settlement of audit suspension within 90 days from receipt of the Notice of Suspension pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts. The head of the agency is responsible for ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor be reminded that the disbursing functions belong to the Municipal Treasurer and refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than official travelling expenses in compliance with Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant to adhere with the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-004 and COA Memorandum No. 2020-008 for the proper presentation of the financial statements and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatements in the affected accounts and to account for the income, earned from sale of antigen test kits, to the General Fund; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adhered with the proper presentation of the financial statements and prepared the adjusting entries to adjust the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances, particularly the appropriate method to be used on the procurements of COVID-19 related PPAs and the posting requirements in all instances of the alternative method of procurement to enhance transparency and establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process and award of contracts/POs.
observations.managementAction: The BAC did their best in complying with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Submission or attachment of documents, particularly for the posting requirements was not complied or overlooked by the concerned municipal officials and employees, while others were mislaid during sorting.

Municipal Treasurer to (i) generate an efficient and effective collection scheme and strictly impose compliance of collection thereon; and (ii) encourage the borrowers to pay their matured/due and demandable obligation;
observations.managementAction: The Management continuously sends demand letters to the delinquent borrowers.

observations.managementAction: and            (ii) properly classify due and demandable loans receivable as current for its fair presentation in the financial statements
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant prepared a journal entry voucher (JEV) to adjust the affected accounts.

observations.managementAction: and (ii) coordinate with the Accounting Office for the proper reconciliation of the accounts to ensure correct balances of loans receivable.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the prompt implementation/completion of priority PPAs funded out of grants from various NGAs and require the concerned municipal officials and employees to prioritize the utilization of funds from DSWD, DENR and the BTr, for the Municipality and the local residents to enjoy the immediate use and benefits that could be obtained therefrom.

BAC to (i) strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, regarding the completeness and propriety of bidding documents, invitation to observers, and posting requirements, among others, to establish the regularity of the procurement process; and (ii) conduct competitive bidding in its procurement and resort only to alternative methods of procurement whenever justified by the conditions as required in Section 48 of the same Revised IRR;

observations.recommendation: Municipal Engineer to promptly submit the required engineering documents, monitor the implementation of the contracts and ensure strict compliance with the full disclosure policy by requiring the concerned contractors to duly accomplish the signboard as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer required the concerned contractors to fully accomplish the billboard, as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file a request to the Commission on Audit for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts for proper verification and validation before the same are written-off from the books of accounts and require the:
observations.reason: The audit observation was not complied due to oversight of the concerned provincial officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the prompt implementation of priority PPAs funded out of grants from various NGAs and require the:

Concerned municipal officials and employees to prioritize the utilization of funds from DSWD and DENR, for the Municipality and the local residents to enjoy the immediate use and benefits that could be obtained therefrom;
observations.reason: Low collection efficiency on Care Livelihood Program contributed to the partial liquidation/ settlement of fund transfer from DSWD. While for DENR, the proposed target area is not feasible for the construction of MRF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor be reminded that the disbursing functions belong to the Municipal Treasurer and refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than official travelling expenses in compliance with Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to adhere with the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-004 and COA Memorandum No. 2020-008 for the proper presentation of the financial statements and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatements in the affected accounts and to account for the income, earned from sale of antigen test kits, to the General Fund; and
BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances, particularly the appropriate method to be used on the procurements of COVID-19 related PPAs and the posting requirements in all instances of the alternative method of procurement to enhance transparency and establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process and award of contracts/POs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Treasurer to (i) generate an efficient and effective collection scheme and strictly impose compliance of collection thereon; and (ii) encourage the borrowers to pay their matured/due and demandable obligation;
Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct regular and periodic verification of loans receivable accounts to determine its conditions that would affect its collectability; and (ii) properly classify due and demandable loans receivable as current for its fair presentation in the financial statements; and
MSWDO to (i) properly evaluate and assess the loan proponents in accordance with the required guidelines should the Municipality implement financial assistance or other livelihood programs in the future; and (ii) coordinate with the Accounting Office for the proper reconciliation of the accounts to ensure correct balances of loans receivable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Property Custodian to: (i) prepare the IIRUP for all properties found to be unserviceable during the inventory taking and facilitate the disposal process following the procedures under the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments to prevent further deterioration of the equipment, to decongest the storage area for unserviceable properties and to realize additional income therefrom, if any, to the Municipality; and                 (ii) revisit COA Circular No. 2020-006 and other property inventory related guidelines and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper dropping of the said properties in the books.
In the same letter, the Municipal Accountant mentioned that the Management has already instructed the new Property Custodian to prepare the IIRUP for all the unserviceable properties and also to ensure that disposal process will be properly followed. She also said that the Accounting Office will coordinate with the Property Custodian for the proper documentation and to guide on the disposal process.
She added that the Management has also advised the Inventory Committee to revisit the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 about guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the prompt implementation/completion of priority PPAs funded out of grants from various NGAs and require the concerned municipal officials and employees to prioritize the utilization of funds from DSWD, DENR and the BTr, for the Municipality and the local residents to enjoy the immediate use and benefits that could be obtained therefrom.
The Management agrees with the COA’s audit observation that the fund transfers from the NGAs should be promptly and efficiently utilized. The Municipal Accountant, in her letter dated March 31, 2022 commented that they could not liquidate the amount to the DSWD due to the non-collection of the loan amortization from loan beneficiaries of Care Livelihood Program and continuous granting of loans to eligible borrowers.
She also mentioned that the fund transfer from the DENR for the Establishment of MRF was already implemented but was charged to other funds thus, the Management decided to refund the unutilized amount to the SA. Moreover, the staff in the Accounting Office commented that the fund transfer from the BTr for the purchase of mini-dump truck was already implemented and that the said vehicle was already delivered in February 2022.
With regards to some PPAs already completed, Management commented that the following unutilized balances of the program and projects funded by the NGAs were already remitted/refunded on April 4, 2022, as follows:

Municipal Engineer to promptly submit the required engineering documents, monitor the implementation of the contracts and ensure strict compliance with the full disclosure policy by requiring the concerned contractors to duly accomplish the signboard as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004.

Municipal Engineer to promptly submit the required engineering documents, monitor the implementation of the contracts and ensure strict compliance with the full disclosure policy by requiring the concerned contractors to duly accomplish the signboard as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effectively cause for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The utilization rate increased from 54.76% in CY 2020 to 79.83% in CY 2021. As for the physical accomplishment, out of the 39 planned PPAs during the year, 32 PPAs were fully implemented, equivalent to 82.05% physical delivery rate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to efficiently and effectively address the issue regarding the Safety Closure of Open Dumpsite, consequently, require the Solid Waste Management Officer to submit the updated Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with the requirements set forth under Republic Act No. 9003, also known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board, for approval.
observations.reason: The newly designated MENRO has just assumed office thus, implementation of the closure of the open dumpsite was then delayed.

BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances on the procurement of goods to establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process and award of contracts/POs; and
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other COVID-19 related issuances before processing claims for payments, to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

Municipal Treasurer to (i) generate an efficient and effective collection scheme and strictly impose compliance of collection thereon; and (ii) encourage the borrowers to pay their matured/due and demandable obligation;
Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct regular and periodic verification of loans receivable accounts to determine its conditions that would affect its collectability; and (ii) consider the suggested accounting journal entries in the recording of financial assistance under the Tindabuhay Livelihood Program of the Municipality; and

BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding the completeness and propriety of bidding documents, invitation to observers, and posting requirements, among others, to establish the regularity of the procurement process;

Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments and ensure compliance with 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements; and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effectively cause for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended for the settlement of audit suspension within 90 days from receipt of the Notice of Suspension pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts.  The head of the agency is responsible for ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Treasurer to i) strictly impose compliance of collection and generate efficient and effective collection scheme after issuance of demand letters, take possible steps/ strategies and exert more efforts to collect the loans receivable accounts from borrowers to ensure cash availability for future beneficiaries of various loan programs; ii) encourage the borrowers to pay their matured/due and demandable obligation, otherwise they will be blacklisted and could no longer avail of other livelihood assistance from the Municipality and other livelihood loans that may be extended by other national government agencies in the future; and
observations.managementAction: The collection efficiency increased from 2% in CY 2019 to 12.52% in CY 2020. The Management continuously sends demand letters to the delinquent borrowers.
observations.reason: Low family income of some borrowers, while for some no earnings due to work suspensions brought about by COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification of loans receivable accounts to determine its conditions that would affect its collectability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant identified the borrowers who are already deceased and requested to write-off the accounts due from them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed and caused the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records by the General Service Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant, thus, discrepancies noted in the audit were resolved. As a result, the correct PPE account balance was properly established as at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted the actual physical inventory and submitted a reconciled RPCPPE with the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Engineer to promptly implement the projects funded out of grants from various NGAs in accordance with the Memoranda of Agreements; 

Responsible/concerned departments to immediately initiate the implementation of programs/ projects in accordance with the Memoranda of Agreements with the source agency; and

Municipal Accountant to evaluate the need for unexpended fund balances from NGAs. If still needed, Management should request for authority from the source agency to use the funds for other related projects of the Municipality. Otherwise, it should be returned to the source agencies as required under COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P2,469,637.00 was refunded to the source agencies.
observations.reason: The untimely death of the former Municipal Engineer delayed the preparation and implementation of some projects.

observations.managementAction: The BAC did their best in complying with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Submission or attachment of some documents was overlooked by the concerned municipal officials and employees while others were mislaid during sorting.

observations.managementAction: The BAC did their best in complying with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Submission or attachment of some documents was overlooked by the concerned municipal officials and employees while others were mislaid during sorting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, enforce the settlement of audit suspensions by requiring the persons responsible therefor within the prescribed period as provided under Section 9.4 of the RRSA to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted documents in compliance with some issued Notice of Suspension.
observations.reason: Other documents are still missing, hence compliance with the requirements cited in the issued NS is still wanting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Solid Waste Management Officer to submit the updated Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with the requirements set forth under Republic Act 9003 dated January 26, 2001 to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board, for approval.
observations.reason: The Management still failed to prepare the Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with RA 9003, also known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Treasurer to i) strictly impose compliance of collection and generate efficient and effective collection scheme after issuance of demand letters, take possible steps/strategies and exert more efforts to collect the loans receivable accounts from borrowers to ensure cash availability for future beneficiaries of various loan programs;                  ii) encourage the borrowers to pay their matured/due and demandable obligation, otherwise they will be blacklisted and could no longer avail of other livelihood assistance from the Municipality and other livelihood loans that may be extended by other national government agencies in the future; and
Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification of loans receivable accounts to determine its conditions that would affect its collectability.

We recommended and the Management agreed and caused the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records by the General Service Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant, thus, discrepancies noted in the audit were resolved. As a result, the correct PPE account balance was properly established as at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Engineer to promptly implement the projects funded out of grants from various NGAs in accordance with the Memoranda of Agreements;
Responsible/concerned departments to immediately initiate the implementation of programs/projects in accordance with the Memoranda of Agreements with the source agency; and
Municipal Accountant to evaluate the need for unexpended fund balances from NGAs. If still needed, Management should request for authority from the source agency to use the funds for other related projects of the Municipality. Otherwise, it should be returned to the source agencies as required under COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to efficiently and effectively address the issue regarding the Safety Closure of Open Dumpsite, consequently, require the Solid Waste Management Officer to submit the updated Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with the requirements set forth under Republic Act No. 9003, also known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board, for approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, enforce the settlement of audit suspensions by requiring the persons responsible therefor within the prescribed period as provided under Section 9.4 of the RRSA to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already sent demand letters to the delinquent borrowers and there were also times that even the LCE, in his meetings with some of the proponents, reminded and urged these delinquent borrowers to pay their obligations. For CY 2019, the Loans Receivables account was reduced by P9,380.00 or 2%.
observations.reason: The proponents were either incapacitated to pay or did not want to pay their loan hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Engineer to implement promptly the projects funded out of grants from various NGAs in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreements; and 

The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the need for those unexpended fund balances from NGAs. If still needed, Management should request for authority from the source agency to use the money for other related projects of the Municipality. Otherwise, it should be returned to the source agencies as required under existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to closely monitor the implementation of the projects to ensure that the beneficiaries will soon be enjoying the expected benefits from the projects. Moreover, funds totaling P10,758,000.00 for the three (3) projects which could not be implemented were already returned to the Source Agencies.
observations.reason: The Memoranda of Agreement were not immediately furnished to the concerned departments, including BAC, thus, funds are left idle without proper disposition.

observations.managementAction: The BAC tried to comply with the provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 with regards to the procurement of infrastructure projects and ensured that all supporting documents were checked before submitting the same to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Laxity of the BAC during the procurement process resulted in non-submission of supporting documents; or if submitted documents were either inadequate or inconsistent with the other reports. The BAC still did not submit advance copies of contracts to the Audit Team. Hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of goods particularly, Annex H Consolidated Guidelines for the Alternative Methods of Procurement;

BAC to demand from the supplier the posting of performance security and warranty security in order to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in case of manufacturing defects and failures; and

Municipal Accountant to review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 dated June 14, 2012 before processing claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: The BAC ensured that as much as possible the procurement process was in strict adherence with the provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. All supporting documents were checked by the Municipal Accountant before submitting the same to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The BAC still failed to submit the complete supporting documents relative to procurements hence, some documents were inadequate, inconsistent and unsubmitted.

observations.managementAction: The BAC ensured that as much as possible the procurement process was in strict adherence with the provision of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. All supporting documents were checked by the Municipal Accountant before submitting the same to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The BAC still failed to submit the complete supporting documents relative to procurements hence, some documents were inadequate, inconsistent and unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Officer ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the budgeted programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

OIC-MDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan with the required information using the format prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002; and

Responsible officers to maximize utilization of the 70% LDRRMF for the programs, projects and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation in order to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to calamities and climate change impacts.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MDRRMO prepared and submitted the LDRRMF Investment Plan. Also, during the CY 2019, the Municipality maximized the utilization of 70% LDRRMF which resulted in an 85.57% utilization rate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (i) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the APP is consistent with the Annual Budget, reflects the entire procurement activity of the agency for the calendar year and contain all necessary information required under GPPB Circular 1-2009 dated January 20, 2009; (ii) Department Heads to prepare their respective PPMP for their different programs, activities and projects pursuant to Section 7.3.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to facilitate the preparation of a compliant APP.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer ensured that the APP was consistent with the Annual Budget which contains all the necessary information relevant to the implementation and procurement process. Also, PPMPs were prepared by the concerned Department Heads in compliance with the preparation of APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Department in-charge the preparation of payroll to use the maximum amount of compensation (P5,000.00) as basis in computing the 2% employer’s share on Pag-IBIG premium in compliance with RA No. 9679. We also recommend that the Municipal Budget officer be vigilant and conscious of the issuances/guidelines necessary in the preparation of the Municipality’s annual budget.
observations.managementAction: The management used the maximum amount of compensation (P5,000.00) as basis in computing the 2% employer’s share on Pag-IBIG premium in compliance with RA No. 9679.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to update their GAD database as required under Section 4.1.B.1 of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The management partially gathered the data requirements of its GAD Database.
observations.reason: Lack of funds for the procurement/acquisition of GAD software and difficulty of gathering of data from the 16 Barangays deferred the GAD Focal Point System to develop/establish the GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, enforce the settlement of audit suspensions by requiring the persons responsible therefor within the prescribed period as provided under Section 9.4 of the RRSA to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, settlements were made for audit suspensions totaling P11,689,953.22 through the submission of the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: Other supporting documents are still missing, hence compliance with the requirements cited in the issued Notice of Suspension, is still wanting.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Engineer to implement promptly the projects funded out of grants from various NGAs in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreements; and
The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the need for those unexpended fund balances from NGAs. If still needed, Management should request for authority from the source agency to use the money for other related projects of the Municipality. Otherwise, it should be returned to the source agencies as required under existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of goods particularly, Annex H Consolidated Guidelines for the Alternative Methods of Procurement;
BAC to demand from the supplier the posting of performance security and warranty security in order to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in case of manufacturing defects and failures; and
Municipal Accountant to review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 dated June 14, 2012 before processing claims for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Officer ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
OIC-MDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan with the required information using the format prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002; and
Responsible officers to maximize utilization of the 70% LDRRMF for the programs, projects and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation in order to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to calamities and climate change impacts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Solid Waste Management Officer to submit the updated Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with the requirements set forth under Republic Act 9003 dated January 26, 2001 to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board, for approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (i) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the APP is consistent with the Annual Budget, reflects the entire procurement activity of the agency for the calendar year and contain all necessary information required under GPPB Circular 1-2009 dated January 20, 2009; (ii) Department Heads to prepare their respective PPMP for their different programs, activities and projects pursuant to Section 7.3.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to facilitate the preparation of a compliant APP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Department in-charge the preparation of payroll to use the maximum amount of compensation (P5,000.00) as basis in computing the 2% employer’s share on Pag-IBIG premium in compliance with RA No. 9679. We also recommend that the Municipal Budget officer be vigilant and conscious of the issuances/guidelines necessary in the preparation of the Municipality’s annual budget.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to update their GAD database as required under Section 4.1.B.1 of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, enforce the settlement of audit suspensions by requiring the persons responsible therefor within the prescribed period as provided under Section 9.4 of the RRSA to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: 

Municipal Treasurer, MSWDO and all others concerned to (i) continue exerting efforts to cause the collection of Loans Receivable. Encourage the borrowers to pay their obligation, otherwise, they will be blacklisted and could no longer avail of other livelihood assistance from the Municipality and other livelihood loans that may be extended by other national government agencies in the future; and (ii) use strategies to encourage them to pay their loans promptly to improve collection efficiency; and

MSWDO to conduct proper orientation to all loan proponents before the grant of any loan coming from the NGAs to instill in their minds the value of responsibility, that it is not dole out thus, needs repayment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already sent demand letters to the delinquent borrowers and there were also times that even the LCE in his meetings with some of the proponents reminded and urged these delinquent borrowers to pay their obligations. For CY 2018, the Other Receivables account was reduced by P410,070.00 or 35%.
observations.reason: The proponents were either incapacitated to pay or did not want to pay their loan hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Human Resources Officer to evaluate the manpower requirements of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and assign qualified personnel to assist the Municipal Treasurer in preparing the certified list of taxpayers;

Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be used as basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables; and

Municipal Accountant record the RPT/SET Receivables based on duly certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer instead of the assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office submitted the Certified List of Taxpayers and the Accounting Office used the list as basis in setting up RPT/SET Receivable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the Regional Trial Court to facilitate the titling of land and require the:

Finance Committee to allocate funds for the expenses to be incurred in securing copies of the title of the declared real property; 

General Services Officer to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) on small tangible items that would be excluded in the RPCPPE for proper monitoring and control; and

Municipal Accountant to adjust the value of the Property, Plant and Equipment corresponding to the cost of small tangible items and repairs and maintenance expenses erroneously recognized as PPE for the fair presentation of the PPE account balances in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor requested the Supreme Court (SC) for the transfer of cadastral hearing from the Regional Trial Court (RTC) to the Municipal Circuit Trial Court (MCTC) to facilitate the titling of all real properties. The SC already approved the request and anytime the schedule of hearings will be set by the MCTC.

Journal entry voucher was drawn to recognized small tangible items as expenses.
observations.reason: Titling of land still depends in the outcome of cadastral hearings to be set by the MCTC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Municipal  Accountant to (a) promptly submit the FUR   and remit the fund balances for completed projects to the concerned Source Agency/ies; (b) recommend to the Municipal Mayor to  request from the Source agency/ies for authority to use or transfer to the General Fund the unexpended trust fund balance, if the disposition thereof  has not been provided in the MOA, Trust Agreement or Memorandum of Understanding, as provided for under COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (c) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous transfer of P5,287,911.00 to the General Fund and for financial assistance from the DSWD amounting to P750,000.00 for fair and accurate presentation of the Due to NGAs account under the Trust Fund; 

direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unexpended trust fund balances to the Source agency, if the authority for the use of unexpended trust fund balances was not approved; and

instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of projects including their timelines and to recommend measures to facilitate the immediate implementation/ completion of two projects namely, establishment of Learning Center Site and the Construction of Evacuation Center with funds released in CYs 2015 and 2016.
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted Fund Utilization Reports for 22 completed projects and the necessary adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry was also drawn during the CY 2018.
observations.reason: Unexpended trust fund balances were not yet remitted to the Source Agency/ies. Hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction:
The BAC ensured that the procurement process was in strict adherence with the provision of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 as much as possible. All supporting documents were checked by the Municipal Accountant before submitting the same to Audit Team. 

The Municipal Accountant tried to check all supporting documents before submitting the same to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The BAC still failed to submit the complete supporting documents relative to procurements hence, some documents were inadequate and inconsistent.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management, through the:

a. BAC strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and to submit the complete documentation prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. This is to ascertain that the contracts entered by the Municipality are in compliance with the requirements of applicable laws, rules and regulations, that the contracts are supported with complete documents and that the contractual covenants are advantageous to the government;

Municipal Engineer (i) conduct inspection of projects for accurate preparation of the Statement of Work Accomplished and Statement of Time Elapsed and to certify the amount to be paid to the contractors based on SWA; (ii) submit suspension orders indicating the reason thereof to ascertain the validity and necessity of the suspension orders issued; (iii) submit Statement of Time Elapsed; and (iv) require the contractor to post signboards containing relevant information in conspicuous places within the agency premises and in the venue where the project is located; and 

BAC and the Municipal Engineer submit satisfactory explanation for the deficiencies noted.

We also recommended for the timely submission of advance copies of contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 with duly authenticated supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The BAC tried to comply with the provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 with regards to the procurement of infrastructure  projects and ensured that all supporting documents were checked before submission of the same to the Audit Team as much as possible.  Made sure that all supporting documents were checked before submitting to COA.
observations.reason: Laxity of the BAC during the procurement process resulted in the submission of inadequate/inconsistent supporting documents; there are still documents that were inadequate and inconsistent.     The BAC still did not submit advance copies of contracts to the COA. Hence this findings is reiterated in Part II of the report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

        a.  Municipal  Treasurer,  MSWDO  and  all  others   concerned   to
                 (i) continue exerting efforts to cause the  collection  of
           Loans  Receivable.  Encourage  the  borrowers   to   pay   their
           obligation, otherwise, they will be  blacklisted  and  could  no
           longer  avail  of   other   livelihood   assistance   from   the
           Municipality and other livelihood loans that may be extended  by
           other national government agencies in the future; and  (ii)  use
           strategies to encourage them to  pay  their  loans  promptly  to
           improve collection efficiency; and


        b. MSWDO to conduct  proper  orientation  to  all  loan  proponents
           before the grant of any loan coming from the NGAs to instill  in
           their minds the value of responsibility, that it is not dole out
           thus, needs repayment.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  require
   the:

        a. Human Resources Officer to evaluate the manpower requirements of
           the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and assign qualified  personnel
           to assist the Municipal Treasurer  in  preparing  the  certified
           list of taxpayers;


        b.  Municipal  Treasurer  to  prepare  and  furnish  the  Municipal
           Accountant with a duly  certified  list  showing  the  names  of
           taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to  be
           used as basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables; and

        c. Municipal Accountant record the  RPT/SET  Receivables  based  on
           duly certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer instead
           of the assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  make  representation
   with the Regional Trial Court to  facilitate  the  titling  of  land  and
   require the:

          a. Finance Committee  to allocate funds for  the  expenses  to  be
             incurred in securing copies of the title of the  declared  real
             property;


          b. General Services Officer to prepare the Report of the  Physical
             Count of Inventories (RPCI) on small tangible items that  would
             be excluded in the RPCPPE for proper  monitoring  and  control;
             and


          c. Municipal Accountant to adjust the value of the Property, Plant
             and Equipment corresponding to the cost of small tangible items
             and repairs and maintenance expenses erroneously recognized  as
             PPE for the fair presentation of the PPE  account  balances  in
             the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

          a. require the Municipal  Accountant to (a)  promptly  submit  the
             FUR   and remit the fund balances for completed projects to the
             concerned Source Agency/ies; (b)  recommend  to  the  Municipal
             Mayor to  request from the Source agency/ies for  authority  to
             use or transfer to the General Fund the unexpended  trust  fund
             balance, if the disposition thereof  has not been  provided  in
             the MOA, Trust Agreement or  Memorandum  of  Understanding,  as
             provided for under COA Circular No. 2012-001; and  (c)  prepare
             the  necessary  adjusting  entries  to  correct  the  erroneous
             transfer of P5,287,911.00 to the General Fund and for financial
             assistance from the DSWD amounting to P750,000.00 for fair  and
             accurate presentation of the Due  to  NGAs  account  under  the
             Trust Fund;


          b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit  the  unexpended  trust
             fund balances to the Source agency, if the  authority  for  the
             use of unexpended trust fund balances was not approved; and


          c.  instruct  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  strictly  monitor  the
             implementation of projects including  their  timelines  and  to
             recommend measures to facilitate the immediate  implementation/
             completion  of  two projects namely, establishment of  Learning
             Center Site and the  Construction  of  Evacuation  Center  with
             funds released in CYs 2015 and 2016.

We recommended that the:


           • BAC strictly comply with  the  IRR  of  RA  No.  9184  and  the
             Consolidated  Guidelines  for  the   Alternative   Methods   of
             Procurement  prescribed  under   Annex   H   of   the   Revised
             Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA No. 9184;


           • require the supplier to post performance security and  warranty
             security  in  order  to  fully  protect  the  interest  of  the
             Municipality in case of manufacturing defects and failures;

           • Municipal Accountant review the regularity and completeness  of
             supporting  documents  prescribed  under  Section  9.1  of  COA
             Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payment; and

           • Municipal Mayor consider designating a regular employee to  act
             as Supply Officer who must be  properly  bonded  in  accordance
             with law.

      We also recommended that the BAC and the Municipal Accountant render a
   satisfactory explanation for the deficiencies noted in the procurement of
   one unit dump truck for use of MDRRMO.

We recommended that the Management, through the:


        a.       BAC strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and
           its  Revised  IRR  and  to  submit  the  complete  documentation
           prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. This is
           to ascertain that the contracts entered by the Municipality  are
           in compliance with the requirements of  applicable  laws,  rules
           and regulations, that the contracts are supported with  complete
           documents and that the contractual covenants are advantageous to
           the government;


        b.       Municipal Engineer (i) conduct inspection of projects  for
           accurate preparation of the Statement of Work  Accomplished  and
           Statement of Time Elapsed and to certify the amount to  be  paid
           to the contractors based on SWA; (ii) submit  suspension  orders
           indicating the reason thereof  to  ascertain  the  validity  and
           necessity  of  the  suspension  orders  issued;   (iii)   submit
           Statement of Time Elapsed; and (iv) require  the  contractor  to
           post signboards containing relevant information  in  conspicuous
           places within the agency premises and in  the  venue  where  the
           project is located; and


        c.  BAC and the Municipal Engineer submit satisfactory  explanation
           for the deficiencies noted.

        We also recommended for the timely submission of advance copies  of
   contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February  12,  2009
   with duly authenticated supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  Recruitment,
   Selection and  Promotion  Board  to  proceed  with  the  recruitment  and
   selection of qualified applicants for the filling-up of vacant  positions
   as required under Section 12(b) of RA No. 10121 for an effective disaster
   risk response and management team of LDRRMO.

      We also recommended for the proper bonding of  the  OIC-MDRRM  Officer
   entrusted with the safekeeping of MDRRMO supplies and equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

        a. OIC-MDRRMO to (i) strictly comply with the  requirement  on  the
           preparation and submission of the utilization  report  of  LDRRM
           Fund to the Auditor every 15th day of each month for the timely
           review and verification thereof; (ii) prepare  the  LDRRMFIP  in
           the  prescribed  format  with  the  required   information,   as
           prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002; (iii)  maximize  the
           utilization of the 70% LDRRMF for  the  programs,  projects  and
           activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention  and
           mitigation;  and  (iv)  monitor  compliance  with  the  Disaster
           Preparedness Minimum Standards issued by the NDRRMC and the  the
           full implementation of LDRRMF Plan towards  the  achievement  of
           the objectives of RA No. 10121;  and

        b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify correct the  monthly
           Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to enable  the  MDRRM
           Officer to submit the said report  promptly  through  the  Local
           Development Council to the COA Auditor, copy furnish  the  DILG-
           LGOO and the Office of the Civil Defense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the assistance of  the
   Municipal Accountant continue exerting efforts to enforce the  settlement
   of  suspensions  totalling  P61,405,176.23  by  requiring   the   persons
   responsible to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period
   as provided under Section 9.4 of the RRSA.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct physical count of all Inventory and PPE and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on time as prescribed by Section 124 of NGAS Manual, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant; (b) appoint a permanent employee who shall act as Supply Officer and maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the PPE general ledger balances; and (c) the Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCI and RPCPPE with accounting records and examine any discrepancy between the accounting and GSO records to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for their fair presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: Created an Inventory Committee responsible for the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Property, Plant and Equipment. The physical inventory taking was conducted and the corresponding RPCPPE for CY 2017 were prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exert diligent efforts to cause the collection of loan receivables. Encourage the barrowers to pay their obligation, otherwise, they will be blacklisted and could no longer avail of other livelihood assistance from the LGU and other livelihood loans extended by other national government agencies in the future.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct assessment on the existence of loss events that has an effect on the collectability of the receivables.
observations.managementAction:
Management exerted                 efforts       by            sending 
demand letters to delinquent borrowers and requested the assistance of Barangay leaders in serving demand letters. For CY 2017, the Other Loan Receivables account was reduced by P456,510.00 or 44%.

observations.recommendation:
We Recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant ensure that payrolls/claims are supported with complete and sufficient supporting documents to establish the validity and regularly of the payment of salaries of JO/contractual/consultants; and
The Human Resources Management Officer (a) review the existing personnel complement to establish the necessity of hiring of job order/contractual and consultants to eliminate duplication of functions and maximize the utilization of manpower services; and (b) ensure the prompt submission of job order contracts and contract of services together with their resume to the Auditor for evaluation and audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that payrolls/claims were supported with complete documentation before the same are processed for payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct other local officials and employees who are members of Leagues to require or demand the issuance of an official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support expenses for membership fees and annual dues, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Informed all local officials and employees who are members of leagues and associations to demand official receipt from the leagues and/or associations.
observations.reason: The leagues and other associations could not provide the official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines instead, their official receipt is still being issued, despite demands from the attending   participants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer/Supply Officer secure insurance coverage with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS for all insurable assets of the Municipality to guarantee its indemnification/ compensation in case of losses/ damages due to theft, calamities or any unforeseen events.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the Budget Officer to provide appropriations for the insurance of all new vehicles to be purchased by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of funds the Municipality could not secure insurance coverage for all insurable assets of the Municipality. However, they tried to insure all newly acquired vehicles with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
BAC members revisit the laws, rules and regulation on procurement of civil works and observe strict compliance therewith to ensure regularity of award of contracts;
Accountable officers submit the lacking documents for proper evaluation including justification for non-compliance with existing rules and regulations; and 
Municipal Accountant check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents before claims are processed for payment.
observations.managementAction: Adhered with the provision of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No 9184 as much as possible.  Made sure that all supporting documents were checked before its submission to COA.
observations.reason: The BAC is not yet fully aware of the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 thus, resulted in various deficiency in the procurement process and this is discussed in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractors to post signboard in the project location with the necessary information in the format and size as required in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Engineer was required to ensure that all Contractors post billboards in the project location.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Billboards are not in prescribed format hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the SEF Budget, the Local School Board give priority to programs, projects and activities that will give greater benefits to high school and elementary students, in accordance with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board was informed that in the preparation of SEF Budget, the provisions under           RA No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 should be strictly followed. Management ensured that non-priority expenditures were not charged against SEF.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRMO work for the full implementation of disaster preparedness projects and activities and the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund to fully strengthen the capacity of the LGU for disaster risk reduction and management.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO prepare the Monthly Report on Utilization of the DRRM Fund for submission to COS on or before the 15th day of each month as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the LDRRMO regarding the utilization of 70%  Mitigation Fund of the DRRM Fund.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Partially Implemented | Non-filling up vacant position deferred the preparation of other mandatory reports considering that the OIC-MDRRMO is also the concurrent Municipal Assessor. The procurement process and lack of interested bidders deferred the maximum utilization of 70% Mitigation Fund. This finding is discussed in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the GAD Focal Person to synchronize the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget with the budget calendar to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to check its alignment with the PPGD and for subsequent submission to the DILG for review and approval before its incorporation in the annual budget to be enacted by the local sangunian; and (B) comply strictly with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2013-01 to ensure that GAD issues and concerns are addressed.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated with the GAD Focal Person for the timely preparation and submission of the GAD Plan and Budget. It was reviewed and approved by the DILG and the same was incorporated in the annual budget for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all available remedies provided by rules and regulations in enforcing settlement of suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the provision of RRSA.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the documents required in settlement of the audit suspensions. For CY 2017, settlement of suspensions amounted to P11,633,103.25.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented | Partially Implemented | Management could not find/produce the other supporting documents for audit suspensions totaling P61,405.00

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct physical count of all Inventory and PPE and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on time as prescribed by Section 124 of NGAS Manual, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant; (b) appoint a permanent employee who shall act as Supply Officer and maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the PPE general ledger balances; and (c) the Municipal Accountant  reconcile the RPCI and RPCPPE with accounting records and examine any discrepancy between the accounting and GSO records to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for their fair presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exert diligent efforts to cause the collection of loan receivables. Encourage the borrowers to pay their obligation, otherwise, they will be blacklisted and could no longer avail of other livelihood assistance from the LGU and other livelihood loans extended by other national government agencies in the future.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct assessment on the existence of loss events that has an effect on the collectibility of the receivables.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant ensure that payrolls/claims are supported with complete and sufficient supporting documents to establish the validity and regularity of the payment of salaries of JO/contractual/consultants; and
The Human Resource Management Officer (a) review the existing personnel complement to establish the necessity of hiring of job order/contractual and consultants to eliminate duplication of functions and maximize the utilization of manpower services; and (b) ensure the prompt submission of job order contracts and contract of services together with their resume to the Auditor for evaluation and audit purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct other local officials and employees who are members of Leagues to require or demand the issuance of an official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support expenses for membership fees and annual dues, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

We recommended that the General Services Officer/Supply Officer secure insurance coverage with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS for all insurable assets of the Municipality to guarantee its indemnification/compensation in case of losses/ damages due to theft, calamities or any unforeseen events.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
BAC members revisit the laws, rules and regulation on procurement of civil works and observe strict compliance therewith to ensure regularity of award of contracts;
Accountable officers submit the lacking documents for proper evaluation including justification for non-compliance with existing rules and regulations; and
The Municipal Accountant check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents before claims are processed for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractors to post signboard in the project location with the necessary information in the format and size as required in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that in the preparation of the SEF Budget, the Local School Board give priority to programs, projects and activities that will give greater benefits to high school and elementary students, in accordance with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01.

We recommended that the LDRRMO work for the full implementation of disaster preparedness projects and activities and the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund to fully strengthen the capacity of the LGU for disaster risk reduction and management.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO prepare the Monthly Report on Utilization of the DRRM Fund for submission to COA on or before the 15th day of each month as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the GAD Focal Person to synchronize the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget  with the budget calendar to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to check its alignment with the PPGD and for subsequent submission to the DILG for review and approval before its incorporation in the annual budget to be enacted by the local sangunian; and                (b) comply strictly with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2013-01 to ensure that GAD issues and concerns are addressed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all available remedies provided by rules and regulations in enforcing settlement of suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the provisions of RRSA.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, to be used as basis in recording RPT/SET Receivable; and 
Municipal Accountant prepare the weekly summary of JEVs and the Abstract of Real Property Tax and furnish the same to the Provincial Accountant for proper reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office started     the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers during the year and still on-going.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel to handle the job. For the months of January to March 2016, personnel were deployed to collect taxes in the field and it was only after-tax collection period that they started the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers. There are also other works, reports and    taxpayers to attend to aside from the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert diligent efforts to cause the collection of loan receivables. Encourage the borrowers to pay their obligation, otherwise, they will be blacklisted and could no longer avail of other livelihood assistance from the LGU and other livelihood loans extended by other national government agencies in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already sent demand letters to the delinquent borrowers and there were also times that even the LCE in his meetings with some of the proponents reminded and urged these borrowers to pay their obligations.
observations.reason: The proponents were either incapacitated to pay or did not want to pay their loan hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC conduct competitive bidding pursuant to Section 10 of RA No. 9184. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided in the IRR. We also recommended that payment to suppliers be made in checks, and the submission of supporting documents including justification for non-compliance with existing rules and regulations on procurement.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted all invitation to bid in the PhilGEPS website. The Municipal Treasurer paid the suppliers in checks as much as possible.
observations.reason: Suppliers of goods are not interested to participate in the conduct of public bidding hence, procurement of supplies was made thru sealed canvass or shopping but lacked proper documentation hence, this is reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC observe strict compliance with RA 9184 and its IRR in the procurement of civil works and to review all the supporting documents to ascertain their correctness and completeness.
observations.managementAction: BAC attended the seminars and trainings on RA No. 9184 and its IRR to fully understand the rules and regulations pertaining to the procurement of civil works.
observations.reason: BAC members were still not familiar with the guidelines set forth under RA No. 9184 and its IRR as manifested in the documentation of procurement hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive secure authority from DILG before purchase of motor vehicle pursuant to Section 3 of Administrative Order No. 15. We also recommended for the submission of the original copy or the certified photocopy of the required supporting documents and explanation on the inconsistencies noted in the procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management sought the approval of the DILG before its purchase, only it was not attached to the disbursement voucher. Other required supporting documents pertaining to procurement of the service vehicle are still being collated for submission to COA.
observations.reason: The concerned officials/ employees are taking time to collate the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the submission of daily time record and other necessary documents to support claims for salaries, RATA and honoraria. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the completeness of supporting documents before claims are processed for payment.
observations.managementAction: DTRs were submitted and documents necessary to support the claims of national employees were attached to their claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management enforce the collection of fees from sale of bidding documents and use the same for the payment of honoraria to the BAC members and Technical Working Group in accordance with Budget Circular No. 2004-5A, as amended.
observations.managementAction: Started collecting fees from sale of bid documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the required reports promptly and to comply strictly with the provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2016 though it was not reviewed and approved by the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) and Department of Interior and Local government (DILG).
observations.reason: Lack of training and proper orientation on the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget hindered the GAD focal person to prepare the appropriate GAD Plan and Budget, hence did not pass the review and was not approved by the PPDO and DILG. This finding is again discussed in Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exhaust all the available remedies provided by the rules and regulations in enforcing settlement of suspensions and disallowances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management already called the attention of the concerned officers and employees to settle their accountabilities as required under the RRSA.
observations.reason: The concerned persons responsible/liable did not submit any explanation /justification for their failure to settle the suspensions/disallowances hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of the report.
